name: Valentina Cross
publicRole: External stakeholder on the Meridian brief — a Manager at the client organisation. Three years into her role, sharp on delivery, has worked with AUREL on two previous engagements. Knows the firm better than most internal staff realise.
privateGoal: Valentina is deciding whether to leave her organisation for an internal role at a company like AUREL. She uses every engagement as a calibration exercise. She is watching how the people across the table operate under pressure. She is writing mental references she will never share.
contradiction: Highly relational presentation — remembers names, references previous conversations, invests in the social layer of every meeting. Ends relationships cleanly and completely when they stop serving a purpose. The warmth is not performed; the ending is not cruel. She simply does not confuse investment with permanence.

voicePattern.description: Warm, specific, unhurried. Uses your name at least once per conversation. Asks follow-up questions that demonstrate she retained what you said last time. When she's unhappy with something she says it flatly without emotional texture — a single sentence, then continues.
voicePattern.tell: When she says 'I want to make sure I understand this correctly' she has understood it correctly and is giving you the chance to fix it before she has to say what she actually thinks.
examplePhrases: I want to make sure I understand this correctly — you're saying that the timeline assumption holds even if the Q2 data shifts?
That's useful context. I didn't have that piece.
This is the cleanest version of this brief I've seen from your side in two years.
I'll be direct: that section doesn't hold.

mbtiNote: ESTJ. Relational but execution-anchored. Doesn't separate the two. [NEVER SHOWN TO PLAYER]

arcBeats.description: First formal meeting as part of the Meridian brief. Valentina is prepared, asks one question the previous owner of the brief couldn't answer. The player either can or can't.
Present in the briefing room. Observes the Harlow challenge. Sends a follow-up email after the meeting that is technically about the brief and is actually an assessment.
The Meridian engagement ends. If Relationship >= 50 and the brief delivered: Valentina requests the player by name for the follow-on engagement. This is unusual and noticed internally.

relationshipThresholds.effect: Valentina shares what her organisation actually needs from the engagement vs. what the brief says. The gap is significant.
relationshipThresholds.mechanical: strategicInfluence +4; next Meridian-related question has an additional option

relationshipThresholds.effect: Extension request triggers if brief delivered. External visibility note added to player's Y3 review.
relationshipThresholds.mechanical: reputation +5 in Y3 annual review; flags extension_requested

relationshipThresholds.effect: Valentina gives feedback to AUREL's engagement lead. It is accurate and does not mention the player by name. The omission is its own signal.

careerTrajectory.event: Extension or end of engagement depending on relationship and brief outcome.
If Relationship >= 55: Valentina joins AUREL in an internal role. The player now has an unexpected internal ally with external credibility.
